themes.title: Reading Confidence
themes.description: Teachers and tutors repeatedly observed students moving from avoidance to engagement. The pattern was consistent: when students felt safe to make mistakes and were met at their level, they began volunteering to read aloud, choosing harder books, and helping peers.
quotes: She used to hide under the desk. Now she asks to read to the whole class.
He went from 'I can't' to 'Can I try the next page?' in about four weeks.

themes.title: Conceptual Understanding
themes.description: Many students had procedural fluency without conceptual grasp - they could compute but not explain. Tutors who used manipulatives, visual models, and open-ended questioning saw students shift from memorization to reasoning.
quotes: I never understood why it worked until we used the blocks. Now it makes sense.
He can explain his thinking to other kids. That's new.

themes.title: Leadership Emergence
themes.description: Quiet or hesitant students who received consistent 1-on-1 support began taking initiative in the classroom - leading small groups, helping peers, sharing their strategies. Tutors created low-stakes opportunities for leadership that transferred to the larger class.
quotes: I never thought she'd volunteer to lead. She's a different kid now.
He started teaching his classmates how he figured it out. That's leadership.
